title: Patricia Doiron
content:
People.ContactEmail pdoiron@mccarthy.ca
- Dalhousie University
Patricia graduated from the Schulich School of Law at Dalhousie University. In law school, she served as a student editor for the Dalhousie Law Journal, competed in the Donald G.H. Bowman Tax Moot in Toronto, sat as a Student Representative on Dalhousie University’s Senate Disciplinary Committee, and worked as a research assistant for two professors.
Before law school, Patricia earned two bachelor’s degrees focused on communication, journalism, and law and society. She wrote her honours thesis on drug treatment courts as alternatives to incarceration. Patricia brings an entrepreneurial mindset to her work, having operated a communications firm during her undergraduate studies, working with clients in retail, food, and magazine publishing. She also has professional experience in the healthcare and non-profit sectors.
Patricia is a passionate advocate with interests in corporate litigation, dispute resolution, restorative justice, and ESG policy. Outside of the office, Patricia enjoys spending her time practicing yoga, running, hiking, and skiing.

- Publication ESG and Sustainability: Key Trends in Canada 2025 March 31, 2026
